title: cieca verbascifolia
intro: Cieca verbascifolia, commonly known as Verbascum verbascifolium, is a plant species in the Scrophulariaceae family. This perennial herb is native to Europe and Western Asia, valued for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Verbascum verbascifolium include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which can be used to treat various conditions such as coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, including expectorant, antitussive, and bronchodilatory effects.
side_effects: Cieca verbascifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of saponins. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can also lead to liver damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding and bruising due to its anticoagulant properties. Additionally, improper use of cieca verbascifolia may trigger allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory problems, in some individuals. In severe cases, misuse of the plant can even cause kidney failure and seizures, especially in people with pre-existing health conditions or taking certain medications.
